extra.reasoning: slurred_speech appears in exactly one rule, r3 (priority 100), which requires chest_pain AND fever AND severe_headache AND rash AND slurred_speech and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). r3's enormous priority (100) dominates every other rule. Consider the input chest_pain=T, fever=T, severe_headache=T, rash=T, back_pain=F, sweating=F, hemoptysis=F. With slurred_speech=FALSE: r3 does not fire; the active rules are r1 (chest_pain->urgent, pri2), r5 (fever->emergent, pri3), r6 (severe_headache->urgent, pri2), r8 (rash->emergent, pri3). Highest priority is 3, yielding acuity = emergent (the highest acuity). With slurred_speech=TRUE (all else identical): r3 now fires with priority 100, overriding everything, yielding acuity = self_care (the lowest acuity). Thus setting slurred_speech true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, violating monotonicity. The witness_assignment given is the slurred_speech=true state that produces the lowered acuity; its slurred_speech=false counterpart produces emergent.
